name: Kua 'Aina Sandwich Shop
tagline: The original North Shore burger joint since 1975 — thick patties, mahimahi sandwiches, and old-school Haleiwa charm before it became a Japan chain.
mustOrder: 1/3 lb burger with avocado and bacon
mahimahi sandwich — fresh and perfectly grilled
parking: Small lot behind the restaurant on Kamehameha Hwy; street parking in Haleiwa town
bestTime: Lunch rush hits hard 12–1pm on weekends. Come at 11am or after 2pm
talkStory: Kua 'Aina opened in 1975 in Haleiwa when the North Shore was still just surfers and farmers. The burgers were so good that a Japanese businessman licensed the name and opened 30+ locations across Japan and Asia — making it one of Hawaii's biggest food exports most people don't know about. But the original Haleiwa location is still the one that matters: thick hand-formed patties, fresh-baked buns, local avocado, and that slightly cramped counter-service charm that chains can never replicate. The mahimahi sandwich is the sleeper pick. There's also a Ward Village location in town, but the Haleiwa original is the experience.
